3
8%
-
-
modeled carbon values removed from depthseries
3
8%
-
-
Perkin Elmer 2400 CHN analyzer
2
6%
-
-
a peat sampler was used for all wetland habitats, except the adjacent uplands where regular soil core was used for soil sampling
2
6%
-
-
Modeled carbon values not archived. These can be requested from the corresponding author or calculated using the Maine-specific relationship described in the Blue Carbon Manual (Howard et al 2014) Samples dried at 60-80 C. Time approximatley 24-48 hours.
2
6%
-
-
compaction quantified when observed
2
6%
-
-
To determine organic matter, samples were combusted at 350°C for 1h followed by 4h at 550°C.
1
3%
-
-
The samples from Caribbean Panama had fraction carbon determined by LOI rather than EA. A subset of 20 samples were also submitted to EA, so that a regression curve based on these data could be used to estimate the fraction carbon of those samples from their LOI. The local regression model develop
21
58%

dating_notes
VALUES:
12
(5%)
MISSING:
254
(95%)
DISTINCT:
10
(4%)
3
25%
-
-
Pieces of woody mangrove material (generally root tissue) picked from core samples were what was dated in each sample.
1
8%
-
-
A box model approach similar to the CRS model was used, but missing excess 210Pb inventories had to be inferred from historic wetland soils in the same area. The box model was based on the annual atmospheric flux of 210Pbxs estimated for the area and calculated from the mean 210Pbxs inventory of six
1
8%
-
-
Mix of dating methods used for each core. 14C measurements were made on particualte OM, dead seagrass sheaths
1
8%
-
-
CF or CRS
1
8%
-
-
supported 210Pb was estimated from the activity of 214Pb
1
8%
-
-
PLUM dating model: Aquino-López, M.A., Blaauw, M., Christen, J.A., Sanderson, N.K., 2018. Bayesian Analysis of 210Pb Dating. Journal of Agricultural, Biological and Environmental Statistics 23, 317-333.
1
8%
-
-
one core (QM 81) was dated and total core age (1850) was listed
3
25%
-

age_depth_model_notes
VALUES:
11
(4%)
MISSING:
255
(96%)
DISTINCT:
7
(3%)
3
27%
-
-
Most cores dated had only one sample dated, and so a simple assumption of linear accretion assuming that the accretion rate equals the depth of the dated sample divided by the age of that sample. Some cores from the Baja Peninsula have each sample dated independently.
2
18%
-
-
For some cores the total 210Pb asymptote was used to represent supported 210Pb in which case the depth sections used as asymptote are noted in core-level notes. Otherwise 214Pb was measured for each sample to represent supported 210Pb.
2
18%
-
-
median age
1

-
PLUM software used
1
9%
-
-
custom box model used
1
9%
-
-
Plum software used
1

-
Methodology citations: 10.1111/j.1467-9876.2008.00623.x; 10.1016/j.quascirev.2008.07.009
Associations
[Only including dataset "DataFrame"]
■ Squares are categorical associations (uncertainty coefficient & correlation ratio) from 0 to 1. The uncertainty coefficient is assymmetrical, (i.e. ROW LABEL values indicate how much they PROVIDE INFORMATION to each LABEL at the TOP).

• Circles are the symmetrical numerical correlations (Pearson's) from -1 to 1. The trivial diagonal is intentionally left blank for clarity.

■ Squares are categorical associations (uncertainty coefficient & correlation ratio) from 0 to 1. The uncertainty coefficient is assymmetrical, (i.e. ROW LABEL values indicate how much they PROVIDE INFORMATION to each LABEL at the TOP).

• Circles are the symmetrical numerical correlations (Pearson's) from -1 to 1. The trivial diagonal is intentionally left blank for clarity.

carbon_profile_notes
MISSING:
230
(86%)
3
8%
-
-
modeled carbon values removed from depthseries
3
8%
-
-
Perkin Elmer 2400 CHN analyzer
2
6%
-
-
a peat sampler was used for all wetland habitats, except the adjacent uplands where regular soil core was used for soil sampling
2
6%
-
-
Modeled carbon values not archived. These can be requested from the corresponding author or calculated using the Maine-specific relationship described in the Blue Carbon Manual (Howard et al 2014) Samples dried at 60-80 C. Time approximatley 24-48 hours.
2
6%
-
-
compaction quantified when observed
2
6%
-
-
To determine organic matter, samples were combusted at 350°C for 1h followed by 4h at 550°C.
1
3%
-
-
The samples from Caribbean Panama had fraction carbon determined by LOI rather than EA. A subset of 20 samples were also submitted to EA, so that a regression curve based on these data could be used to estimate the fraction carbon of those samples from their LOI. The local regression model develop
1
3%
-
-
Sub-samples prepped and run through the elemental analyzer were dried at 65ºC until constant mass was reached.
1
3%
-
-
not all samples could be run on an elemental analyzer. For samples collected at Rush Ranch, Coon Island, and China Camp, the LOI to %OC equation from Callaway et al. 2012 was used; for all other modeled %OC values, a LOI to %OC relationship was determined from current study data and is included with
1
3%
-
-
Deltaic setting, soils were very organic.
1
3%
-
-
DBD was estimated as the dry mass of the slice divided the core volume sampled. The core volume sampled was estimated as the product of the core tube area (pi*r2) and the slicing-thickness (h); sample mass used for LOI ranged from 0.5 to 1g; estimated fraction organic carbon calculated using LOI% to
1
3%
-
-
OC measured from modified dichromate oxidation method of Bajgai et al 2013 (https://hdl.handle.net/10018/1015118)
1
3%
-
-
Tested for CO3 using HCl, no presence observed
1
3%
-
-
%LOI < 0.2: %Corg = 0.40 x %LOI - 0.21; %LOI > 0.2: %Corg = 0.43 x %LOI - 0.33
1
3%
-
-
Dried and weighed core sections were analyzed at the University of California, Davis Analytical Laboratory, using acid fumigation and dynamic flash combustion following methods described in Harris and others (2001) and the Association of Official Analytical Chemists (1997) Official Method 972.43
1
3%
-
-
Most of the samples from the Galapagos did not receive any treatment to remove carbonate before measurement of fraction carbon, because of their origin in low carbonate soil, with the exeption of nine samples from the Galapagos that did undergo carbonate removal did so because they visibly contained
1
3%
-
-
total carbon (unashed) and inorganic (ashed) carbon determined via EA and organic carbon calculated from the difference
1
3%
-
-
Samples dried at 60-80 C. Time approximately 24-48 hours.
1
3%
-
-
organic C was calculated as the difference between inorganic C and total C
1
3%
-
-
fraction carbon was modeled using the Kauffman 2011 equation
1
3%
-
-
Samples dried over 36-48hrs for DBD. LOI determined from a 3-5g subsample of each original dry, ground, sample. If no carbonates were present in original soil sample, 2-3mg were sent for EA. If carbonates were present, 15-20mg were sent for EA to allow for total carbon and post-LOI EA.
1
3%
-
-
dry bulk density time from 48-72 hours
1
3%
-
-
For all samples, increments were wet sieved to 64 µm to separate fine and course components the course components were then dry sieved to 500 µm.
1
3%
-
-
fumigated to remove microbeal biomass
1
3%
-
-
Modeled fraction carbon values available in https://doi.org/10.25573/data.10005248
1
3%
-
-
ECS 4010 elemental analyser (Costech Analytical Technologies)
1
3%
-
-
A 3-cm diameter Eijkelkamp gouge auger was used to remove the first 20 cm of soil at each core location. The gouge auger has an open-face design that allows it to be inserted without compacting soils. Each core was visually examined at the time of collection, and no signs of compaction were obs
1
3%
-
-
10.2 cm inner diameter pvc pipe driven to depth

dating_notes
MISSING:
254
(95%)
3
25%
-
-
Pieces of woody mangrove material (generally root tissue) picked from core samples were what was dated in each sample.
1
8%
-
-
A box model approach similar to the CRS model was used, but missing excess 210Pb inventories had to be inferred from historic wetland soils in the same area. The box model was based on the annual atmospheric flux of 210Pbxs estimated for the area and calculated from the mean 210Pbxs inventory of six
1
8%
-
-
Mix of dating methods used for each core. 14C measurements were made on particualte OM, dead seagrass sheaths
1
8%
-
-
CF or CRS
1
8%
-
-
supported 210Pb was estimated from the activity of 214Pb
1
8%
-
-
PLUM dating model: Aquino-López, M.A., Blaauw, M., Christen, J.A., Sanderson, N.K., 2018. Bayesian Analysis of 210Pb Dating. Journal of Agricultural, Biological and Environmental Statistics 23, 317-333.
1
8%
-
-
one core (QM 81) was dated and total core age (1850) was listed
1
8%
-
-
activities collected to the detection limit
1
8%
-
-
Ra226 measured by gamma via granddaughters Pb—214, Bi-214
1
8%
-
-
CRC model used to determine age-dependent rates of soil accretion for each section of soil cores. CIC model used to calculate a whole-core accretion rate using Pb210 activities.

age_depth_model_notes
MISSING:
255
(96%)
3
27%
-
-
Most cores dated had only one sample dated, and so a simple assumption of linear accretion assuming that the accretion rate equals the depth of the dated sample divided by the age of that sample. Some cores from the Baja Peninsula have each sample dated independently.
2
18%
-
-
For some cores the total 210Pb asymptote was used to represent supported 210Pb in which case the depth sections used as asymptote are noted in core-level notes. Otherwise 214Pb was measured for each sample to represent supported 210Pb.
2
18%
-
-
median age
1
9%
-
-
PLUM software used
1
9%
-
-
custom box model used
1
9%
-
-
Plum software used
1
9%
-
-
Methodology citations: 10.1111/j.1467-9876.2008.00623.x; 10.1016/j.quascirev.2008.07.009
